reviews.review_text: I play multiplayer with my daughter (cross play - me on my PC and my daughter on her Xbox) and it is an absolute  blast. As a parent, tbh some games I play with her just aren't fun to me (Minecraft, for example) but this game is not like that -- it is loads of fun. This game is top tier - the gameplay, combat, and especially the base building with the pals is a lot of fun. There's nothing I would recommend more for a family multiplayer game!
I attacked a bird and the game turned into Dark Souls, 10 out of 10 Pokémon
This game is gonna get a huge update on July 10 and its gonna be Peak as ♥♥♥♥. Nintendo could never compare, greedy ass bastards lol.

I'm a sucker for monster taming games (spent 600~ hours on temtem as well). Pokémon ain't ever coming to pc, so I'm gonna take what I can get, and I hope that palworld will encourage more devs to release games of that genre. Just like stradew opened a floodgate to farming sims.

I wanted this game since it released, but only got a good enough laptop to run it as of recent. 
Discounted and heavily updated. With all the past updates introducing raids, new islands, QoL, new pals, new mechanics,crossplay,terraria collab, all the bug and balance fixes with 1.0 release right around the corner with even more content awaiting.

At first I was sceptical if I would even enjoy this game alone, sandbox games can be a massive hit or miss for me. However before I realised I was getting addicted.

 I instantly realised how wrong it was to call it "Pokémon with guns". This game is soooo different. 

In palworld 
-You spawn on an island, half naked, with nothing. You see a message "The towers are the key, the tree holds the truth "
-You beat up pals yourself and catch them
-You build your base, craft everything
-You complete tasks, and uncover the lore

You don't spawn in a town, as a 10 year old getting his starter, beating gym leaders and random trainers along the way, just to beat up the big 3.  
 
~~~~~~~~~
★Japanese learning☆

It's a big reason why I picked this game up in the first place. I wanted a crafting game without too much yapping so that I could focus on memorising crafting materials/tools/weapons in Japanese,this game is made by Japanese Devs which is a massive bonus point as well. 
There's so many fun wordplays in the pal names.

I still have so much left to explore and do, I love this game!

Palworld is a nice little game. It's ARK with Pokemon. I bought the game because I heard many great things about it, and I remember people loving the game when it first launched.

Unfortunately, I have quite a few problems with the game. IMO there are a lot of baffling decisions when it comes to the difficulty, even if "oh it's fine though cause you can change it at will". I find this is still worthy of criticism because the default settings themselves don't feel balanced, so you need to tweak things slowly until you find what works for you. That, or you look up an online guide that says "these are the best palworld settings for maximum fun!" but fun is subjective...

The music is perfectly mediocre. Not much else to say about it lol

Quests are a nice little checkmark, but the lack of any real interconnected quest or side story makes it feel empty. It would've been nice if the game had quest chains with written stories, but nah, they're all individual.

The "main story" or "main objective" to defeat the towers is... serviceable. The unfortunate thing is that the game doesn't eventually develop into a mood of "you're now in the final area! You've almost beaten the game!". Things just feel the same, all the time. The only difference is that now you can unlock things in the upgrade tree... yay? I've yet to defeat the Feybreak Tower, but part of me believes me that the game will not have any closure or sense of finality once I do that. You may argue that the game is still in early access.. but c'mon. That's just cope.

The combat itself is really whatever. Player interactivity feels practically absent: Because Player weapons feel so weak in general, the only thing you're left to do in combat is use items, switch Pals, and dodge attacks. This makes Palworld feel more like an auto-clicker/afk.

One more problem I can point out is the grinding is so monotonous in the late game. Yes, change the settings... But if your stuff is underleveled, there's isn't a fun way to level up your Pals if you're trying to get from 60 to 65. This problem is highlighted even more when you realize the combat is just a dodge/switch Pal loop.

--

For the positives though, and why Palworld may be good for you..
- You like optimizing teams
- You like exploring
- The game genuinely feels like an "Open world Pokemon game" at times
- You like breeding
- You like collecting 
- You like optimizing workflow in a factory setting

To conclude, I've had my fun with the game, but I find it hard to believe there isn't a better game out there than what Palworld has to offer. The fact that I got mislead by the people who called it "Pokemon with guns" when it's actually "ARK with a pokemon mod" feels saddening, because I would've thought the game had more stuff to do with the Pals other than labor.  

You could say that "Palworld just isn't for me", and that might be true. I do like my games to have a sense of direction and finality to them, like how you can still explore and do things in Elden Ring after you've defeated the final boss (which you know is the final boss due to the setup).

At the very least, when it comes to the Price per Playtime, Palworld is definitely worth buying if you value such a metric.

A lot of fun grind and mechanics, even with bugs like stucking in textures it has too much pros to be bad just because of it, hope they will keep making updates after 1.0 
11/10

Palworld feels like a game created by people who asked, "What if we combined creature collecting, survival crafting, automation, exploration, base building, and multiplayer chaos?"—and then refused to remove any of those ideas.

The result is a wonderfully unpredictable adventure that constantly generates memorable moments.

It may not be the most refined survival game available, but it is one of the most imaginative. Whether you're capturing rare Pals, constructing massive factories, or exploring distant regions with friends, Palworld delivers a sense of freedom and discovery that keeps pulling you back for one more adventure.

Overall 9/10, -1 due to Pals can't evolve

Playing this with friends was a blast the first time and hopefully with time we'll play again at full release. Many thanks for making playing a game actually fun again.

If you spent 200, 300, 500+ hours and want to tell me you don't recommend it. I'm calling BS and suggest either Nintendo or Israel has some blackmail material on you. Perhaps you were on Epstein Island with Trump.

As it turns out pokemon + guns IS visually and thematically distinct!  Plus it's a lot of fun.  I thought I was maybe a bit old for a pokemon clone, but this combines a classic rpg format (hunt and train monsters) with all sorts of fun things like slave labour, organ harvesting, and surprisingly - climbing.

Yeah you can just climb anything like it's some sort of anime Daggerfall.  You can even make a terrifying hobgoblin of a character in a true nod to other Bethesda classics.  Oh, and you can go fishing too.  Suck it, Nintendo.
It's a fun and unique game so far. There are a few frame drops in boss dungeons, which needs to be fixed but otherwise it's been enjoyable.

I think most people who enjoy the survival, craft, base building style of gameplay loops will enjoy this.  So anyone into Ark, Valheim, etc will probably enjoy this too.  If you don't enjoy those games and are looking at it because you're into monster collecting games like Pokemon there's a pretty solid chance you won't actually enjoy this one, because the focus is much more on that exploring, surviving and crafting incrementally better stuff gameplay loop - the Pals just facilitate and enhance that loop.

So if you're looking at this purely because you're a fan of Pokemon, I'd say watch a few gameplay vids or streams to see if the bulk of the gameplay appeals to you, because at it's heart it's about exploring, upgrading and base building, not the monsters (Pals).

The definitive "Pokémon with guns" fever dream you never knew you needed.

Palworld is essentially the chaotic, unfiltered answer to every fan who ever wondered what would happen if you gave a Pikachu an AK-47. It is the only game on the market that leans so heavily into the absurdity of its premise, and honestly, the mechanics are surprisingly solid. While the story is almost non-existent and largely uninteresting, you aren’t here for a deep narrative journey. You’re here for the sheer, ridiculous fun of building a base, crafting high-powered weaponry, and watching your adorable "Pals" help you automate a factory or defend your territory with heavy artillery. It’s a sandbox of pure mayhem that is incredibly easy to sink hours into.

However, it’s worth noting that the game—and especially its marketing—can get pretty dark and "questionable." While the gameplay is addictive, the tonal shift toward graphic brutality can be jarring. If you’ve seen the ads on Instagram where Pals are being brutally chopped or stabbed, you know exactly what I mean. It pushes the boundaries of the monster-taming genre into a territory that feels unnecessarily cruel at times. It’s definitely not the wholesome "catch 'em all" experience most are used to; it’s a much grittier, more cynical take on survival that might leave some players feeling a bit uneasy about the ethics of their digital labor force.

At the end of the day, Palworld is a game that knows exactly what its niche is. If you can look past the lackluster story and the occasionally disturbing visuals, the core gameplay loop is genuinely rewarding and high-energy. It’s a perfect "cup of tea" for players who are bored of the traditional, safe monster-collecting formula and want to see what happens when survival crafting meets ballistic warfare. It’s weird, it’s graphic, and it’s arguably a bit unhinged, but it’s an experience you won't find anywhere else.

8/10 — Come for the cute creatures, stay for the tactical assault lamb.

Suddenly, one day, I decided to play Palworld because it was lying in my library for a while now, untouched, unloved.
If the Chinese Gamedev Community has mastered any art it would be the art of copying. Not a single original idea or mechanic is presented in Palworld that hasn't been done elsewhere. It's a mix of Automation games such as Factorio or Satisfactory. Base building, crafting and weapons taken straight from Rust, Ark or Valheim. 
Despite all of the jank present in this game; seemingly stolen animations or at the very least free assets from unity or unreal engine, questionable models and textures, this game is very fun to play. Huge world, many pals to catch and build, tons and tons of recipes for craft, a lot of content to keep you busy basically.
If you have spare $$$ then buy this game, it's the kind of game Gamefreak would dream of making to such a scale.

I stayed off this game at first as I thought it was a pokemon rip off. Yet, after researching and know more about the lawsuit, I decided to try as I was bored as f and I have friends to play with at that time. 

I was genuinely suprisied how good this game is.

Graphics Sublime. Map was Great despite not fully open yet. Nintendo shame on you. Money grabbing for years and still can't make a well run pokemon game.

The Pals were not the most creative as you might have known by watching videos. Yet, the mechanics of this game is just everything we wanted from a pokemon game. Throwing the spheres, mounting and controlling our pals. Using our pals' abilities.  etc etc. I think the game did a great job to satisfy the OG pokemon players by implementing such a immersive system to fight and bond with our pals. 

The baseworking pal system was also a good design eventhough turning my pals into slavery was never on my bucketlist. It add depths to the pals and also set a different direction to normal pokemon experience.

The building system was the most suprising. I am pretty bad at it but if you have ever look palworld builds on Youtube. So much you can do with this building system.

For now, I am waiting for the 1.0 version to start and learn the game all over again. 

Eventhough there migh be a lot of controversies surrounding this game, you can't denie that this is an amazing game. 

Pokemon Scarlet and Violet was the first everpokemon game that I actually play. I did manage to play til the end (without DLC). Yet, I have fight against the toaster graphics of my switch and kindergarden main story to finsh the game. The graphics and open world system is enough for Palworld to surpass Pokemon recent titles including legend series,

Pros:
-Really solid game overall, even in early access
-Works great on Steamdeck
-Graphics are beautiful, especially the night sky
-The gameplay is very straightforward and provides little barrier to entry
-There’s so much to do in the game that it has something for everyone. Wanna have a chill time? You can. Wanna hardcore battle? You can. Wanna decorate a house you made on a mountain by a lake? You can. Wanna do crime? You absolutely can lol

Cons:
-500+ hours in and I have no idea what the plot is
-Having multiple pals working in the same location causes the SteamDeck to drop frames
-There are spots on the map where you can glitch into the ground and get stuck. I don’t recommend playing on higher levels because of this and the death penalty
-Currently the end game (in early access) is unbalanced for a solo player. I have no one playing this with me and it’s a hell of a grind to get enough to do the final bits
-Are we gonna talk about catching humans in the spheres or……….?


In Summary:
Every dollar you spend on a game should be at least 1 hour of entertainment. This game is an absolute steal for the price and I would recommend it to anyone who wants a fun time running around catching Pals and daydreaming.

Early access jank
Occasional crashes, weird physics, Pals getting stuck, frame drops in huge bases, minor sync issues in multiplayer. Nothing game‑breaking now, but it’s definitely not polished. If you hate jank, wait for full release.

No story, no direction
Pure sandbox, no narrative, no clear “end”. You make your own goals. If you want a guided, story‑driven experience, look elsewhere.

Balance is uneven
Some Pals and abilities are clearly far stronger than others, so late‑game builds tend to converge on the same few options. Raids can also be frustrating — sometimes too easy, sometimes cheap and annoying rather than challenging.
Palworld is messy, ambitious, rough, and brilliant all at once. It’s not finished, but it’s already great — and getting better every month. If you’re willing to put the time in, it gives back tenfold.

Would I pay full price again tomorrow? Absolutely

When I first picked up Palworld, I wasn't entirely sure what to expect.

What I got was 208 hours of one of the most entertaining sandbox survival experiences I've played in recent years.

Palworld is one of those games that constantly gives you something to do. One moment you're exploring a new area, the next you're building up your base, catching Pals, optimizing production lines, breeding for better traits, or getting completely sidetracked by something you hadn't planned on doing at all.

And somehow, that's where a lot of the fun comes from.

The game embraces chaos in the best possible way. It doesn't take itself too seriously, and because of that, it creates countless memorable moments. Whether it was discovering a new Pal, expanding my base, or watching my carefully organized plans immediately fall apart, there was always something happening that made me want to keep playing.

What surprised me most was how addictive the progression loop became. Every upgrade felt meaningful. Every new Pal opened up new possibilities. Every expedition felt like it could lead to something useful or unexpected.

Before I knew it, I had already sunk hundreds of hours into the game.

Of course, it's still an early-access game, and there are areas where that shows. There are rough edges, systems that could be expanded, and plenty of room for improvement. But even with those limitations, I had a fantastic time.

More importantly, the game already provides something a lot of bigger-budget titles struggle to deliver:

It makes me want to come back.

I haven't been playing recently, but that's less about the game and more about having other games competing for my time. Palworld is one of those titles that's always sitting in the back of my mind as something I'll happily revisit when the mood strikes.

And with Version 1.0 still ahead, I'm genuinely excited to see where the game goes from here.

If my first 208 hours are anything to go by, I have a feeling there are many more waiting for me down the road.

Score: 9/10

"208 hours later, Palworld gave me exactly what I wanted: a fun game that never stopped giving me reasons to keep playing. Now I'm just waiting to see what Version 1.0 brings."

Simple Overview:
Overall Score - 8.8/10 
Personal Score - 9.7/10
Visuals - Simple, but clean and pleasing to look at. Can look very gorgeous at times.
Story/Narrative - Basically none, its your world to explore and thrive in.
Audio - Fits the mood of the game, but nothing to write home about. Would love to see more music and ambiance to immerse you.
Gameplay - Addiciting, so much to do, so much to explore. Not overwhelming and easy to grasp. Scratches that itch of so many genres. What more could you want from a creature collecting, base building, GET BACK TO WORK life!
Notes - Cute pals, cool pals, into the box forever pals. Hard-working pals, lazy pals, its just a great game with just enough depth to drag you in and keep you there while also not suffocating you. Its super addicting.

Blabbering:
Palworld really takes amazing aspects from many games and blends them into something fresh and not overwhelming at all. Touching on ideas from Pokemon, Ark, Satisfactory, and more, Palworld is fairly simple but addicting.  It really hits the spot for my creature collecting, base building, adventure seeking, and minmaxing-self. It also has amazing world settings to tailor your adventure for your needs, which is very useful for replayabliity as well as for getting a balanced/well-paced feel for anyone. Plus, its still not even fully out yet, lending itself a wonderful foundation to grow upon.
For those who are sceptical.... Just buy it! I was sceptical too at first

While that is an option for those who like it, I prefer taking a more active role in both the battles and base building aspects of the game! Palworld allows for multiple game styles and for that I am very thankful! This game is, by far, the best 'creature collector' game I have come across thus far!

Palworld is a beautiful clusterfuff of a game that blatantly lifts countless mechanics, tropes, and stylizations from mainly an unmentionable lawsuit-happy company, and I'll be damned if it all doesn't somehow work. It's a comedically brutal and amusingly realistic take on creature collecting merged with a number-go-up automation and survival game. Co-op is good fun as it lets you effectively tackle this multifaceted game as you diversify your crew and take turns exploring, building, and maintaining your ethically questionable ranch. Setting new goals and pursuing them is addictively rewarding when there are so many avenues to engage with. With 1.0 on the horizon, I’d give it a bit, but do really keep an eye on Palworld. It’s a genuine hoot with friends and the most entertaining, charmingly scuffed, and morally uncomfortable adorable animal unpaid labor simulator to grace the gaming landscape.

Game is amazing for survival and building so far. Progression for main story/base building, as well as side quests you find by exploring new villages, etc. There are bad guys ya gotta deal with as well as bosses. Crafting up the yin-yang. You can go melee, or different guns/shotguns, etc, or bow/arrow, etc. Companions up the booty... Enough said: Buy the game!

I love this game. i had it on xbox before thanks to a friend buying it for me and i just decided to make things easier and get the game on steam. i prefer steam over xbox and it lets me have all my games in one place this way. if I had to say something bad about it it would be that there are multiple bugs that make the game feel a little off. not a major complaint just something to say. i love the gameplay and the pals are amazing plus they actually listen to the players, so i highly recommend this game enough so that i have 3 copies of it one on xbox one on my dads family account and one on my account. people say put your money where your mouth is and i have. also worth noting i had well over 7 full days of play time on the xbox copy so i know ill be playing for a long time. and sorry if this is difficult to read i am wrighting this while i am sick and not thinking the best

thank you for your time

This is the Pokémon game of my dreams. I am so sorry Nintendo you are the weakest link, good bye. I've dropped 13 hours into Palworld in just 2 days and I am having a blast. Take the base building of say Enshrouded or Valhiem and combine it with the creature collecting of Pokémon and you have a winner of a game. Plus you can get your Tanzee and Lifmunk a gun to destroy your enemies. What's not to love? I can't wait to drop a couple hundred hours into this.

I would highly recommend this game. I love that there are so many things you can do in this game. It's not just all the cool pals you can catch, but there's  base building and a hugh world to explore. I can't wait for the full game to drop.

Amazing game i love it. Been playing it on xbox ever since it came to early access and now on pc. So much content already, i cant imagine what 1.0 will add. Overall, bye this game if you have a bunch of free time and love base building/monster catching. Ill give my next review when 1.0 comes out.

I highly enjoy this game, love catching pals to battle with and help me improve the base. Building is pretty easy and they've got fun items to decorate with. The pals helping around the base make it feel more lively and busy :)

It's been years since i was so excited and dedicated to a game, in a level of i can't wait to get there/to level type of excitment!

It's a whole bag of FUN with decent people : )
It's like Ark Survival had a baby with Pokemon XD
GOTTA CATCH EM' ALL <3

Xbox Game Pass had this title before MicroScum almost doubled the price on gamepass. Could not afford it. Have a PC and bought cheaper than that on discount. Game plays so much better and mods helps. Fun creature collecting, exploring and base building. Enjoyable experience all round.

i love this game, it's amazing! however, i don't agree with the advertisment and handling of the game. the only way to get pals is to harm them. it's pretty brutal towards docile pals. on another note, i do not like the consistant usage of abusing pals. but hey, to each their own. it's a game. (sorry for spelling errors)

Imagine a Pocket Monster Manager with Guns, Basebuilding and Ressource Managment. Nintendo messed up and this studio gave the genre the RIGHT kick! Pay for INNOVATION instead to a dead monopoly. Thx a ton for this game <3

The game is AMAZING! It's self-paced so you don't feel rushed, you're able to set up a base within a couple of hours (give or take I wasn't paying attention to the time), and you don't need to worry about your pals dying from starvation since they can feed themselves (as long as you have the pal feeding basket). Again, the game is amazing, definitely worth the 30 dollars (or less if a sale's going on).

Palworld invites players into a vibrant, expansive open world where survival crafting seamlessly collides with creature collection. This colorful adventure blends deep base-building, resource management, and exploration with an addictive loop of capturing and utilizing diverse creatures known as Pals to automate your production lines or fight by your side.

It was a deep dive into the game's incredibly addictive progression systems. We spent countless hours gathering resources, optimizing our crafting setups, and diving heavily into the complex breeding mechanics to hatch the ultimate Pals with perfect, optimal perks. While the initial magic eventually faded once we hit the endgame limits, the experience was an absolute blast while it lasted, especially when conquering the world and building up our base as a coordinated group. It is an incredibly fun survival experience that delivers tons of entertainment before you finally step away from the ranch.
Great game. Very fun. Amazing way to kill some time. Palworld has everything. It has guns, animals, exploration, building.
This is a great game it look great, it fun to catch and shot pals. I love to have pals work on my land, I was sad when I butcher a pal for stuff I needed

This game is a wonderful combination of some of my favourite games. The fights are a little easy, but the game is always fun and shows its creativity in many ways. If you liked ARK, you will enjoy this.

best pokemon themed survival game ever. I would recommend this game below 1000rupees only because you will feel bored after 2 months if you gonna play singleplayer, but if u have friends then this will gonna blow your mind
